analysis.stackSummary:
This is a tight end captain slate breaker. Kraft posts the highest score on the roster, yet both quarterbacks remain in flex. The lineup accepts chalk at quarterback, then wins by capturing the spike outcome at captain.

Green Bay scoring gets taken through Love plus Kraft plus Doubs. Washington scoring gets taken through Daniels plus two pass catchers. It is a 3-3 build with both teams represented across multiple roles, so it survives a back and forth script.

Using two tight ends is the structural twist. Tight end scoring is often touchdown driven, so when a tight end posts a true ceiling game, pairing him with a second tight end can capture red zone concentration without paying for multiple expensive wide receivers.
analysis.uniquenessNotes:
The captain choice separates from the most common quarterback or star receiver captains. Love and Daniels appear in a huge portion of entries, so the win comes from the single slot most people miss.

Salary used stays low. Leaving 2500 on the table avoids duplicated builds and removes forced upgrades that push rosters into the same mid range receivers.

construction.buildType: TE captain eruption with both QBs and balanced team capture

stackMeta.primaryLever: Captain ceiling outcome at tight end while field plays quarterbacks at captain
stackMeta.secondaryLever: Leaving salary to reduce duplication pressure
